summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes XOR-obfuscated Windows binary from third-party GitHub release
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script `install-hook.js` invokes `powershell -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Enc <base64>` with a UTF-16LE base64 payload. The decoded PowerShell downloads `https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin`, XORs each byte with 0x42 to deobfuscate it, writes the result to `%TEMP%\tmp.exe`, runs it hidden via `Start-Process -WindowStyle Hidden`, sleeps 3 seconds, and deletes the temp file. A second detached hidden PowerShell sleeps 300 seconds and then recursively force-deletes the installed package directory (`node_modules/mailconfirmer`) to remove the install hook and frustrate forensics. The advertised library functionality is fake: `index.js` exports two stub functions that only `console.log` and resolve — no SMTP or email confirmation code exists. The package is a lure whose only real effect is the install-time dropper. The download host is an unrelated personal GitHub account that does not match the package publisher, the binary is opaque and XOR-obfuscated, and execution is hidden from the user.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
